note: Day 22 (Easter Saturday). 3 new notes added (leads 57, 59, 60) + 1 blocked placeholder (lead 58). Notes for leads 4, 6, 8 removed from active queue — sequences complete or ending today. All 13 carried-forward notes remain GENERIC — linkedin_profiles.json has NEVER been received from HANDS across FIVE consecutive BRAIN runs. Generic notes are still worth sending at this pre-breakup stage: even a 10-15% acceptance rate provides a parallel touch before the sequence closes. Post_reference upgrades are the highest-impact improvement available — upgrade candidates in order: 44 (Dr. Rowe), 42 (Sarah), 60 (James Hewitt), 52 (Linda Walsh), 55 (Craig Lawson). Execution window: 2026-04-22 onwards.

strategy.tone: slightly awkward genuine compliment — stands out from polished AI outreach
anti_patterns: Do not use 'I came across your profile'
Do not use 'I was impressed by'
Do not mention AI or automation
Do not pitch in the note — curiosity only
Do not use 'reach out' or 'touch base'
Do not mention 'digital marketing' or 'web design' explicitly
strategy.upgrade_path: Once profile data available from HANDS (linkedin_profiles.json): replace generic with post_reference (highest impact) or headline type. HANDS: write data/linkedin_profiles.json after each search — include last_post_date and last_post_text (first 200 chars) so BRAIN can judge upgrade potential.

summary.escalation: HANDS has not returned linkedin_profiles.json for FIVE consecutive BRAIN runs. All 13 active notes are generic. Post_reference upgrades achieve ~3-4x higher connection acceptance rates. This is the single highest-impact improvement available to the LinkedIn channel at zero cost. HANDS: after each search, write data/linkedin_profiles.json. Format: {lead_id, linkedin_url, headline, last_post_date, last_post_text (first 200 chars), connection_status, active_within_60_days}.
